feedback: Clean package. Both drafts are on-topic, voice-compliant, and sourced correctly. The $8K personal spend comparison is the best line in either draft — specific, traceable, and genuinely human. LinkedIn has good paragraph asymmetry and a strong rhetorical framework. Bluesky is punchy and appropriately short. No banned words, no em dashes, no throat-clearing, no kill switches triggered. Art is absent (noted, not auto-fail). LinkedIn's closing rhetorical question is a minor style warning — docked one point from structural_variation — but it's not the transition-device pattern, it reads as a genuine open question.
notes: Art placeholder: no editorial illustration was provided. Noted per rubric, not auto-fail. LinkedIn only links to mindpattern.ai, not directly to Variety — acceptable since mindpattern.ai is the content hub and the specific numbers are traceable to the brief's primary source.
